bibliographic_data.title: Analysis of the Penn Korean Universal Dependency Treebank (PKT-UD): Manual Revision to Build Robust Parsing Model in Korean
bibliographic_data.abstract: In this paper, we first open on important issues regarding the Penn Korean Universal Treebank (PKT-UD) and address these issues by revising the entire corpus manually with the aim of producing cleaner UD annotations that are more faithful to Korean grammar. For compatibility to the rest of UD corpora, we follow the UDv2 guidelines, and extensively revise the part-of-speech tags and the dependency relations to reflect morphological features and flexible word-order aspects in Korean. The original and the revised versions of PKT-UD are experimented with transformer-based parsing models using biafﬁne attention. The parsing model trained on the revised corpus shows a significant improvement of 3.0% in labeled attachment score over the model trained on the previous corpus.

core_content.novelty_claim: The paper addresses and revises issues in the Penn Korean Universal Dependency Treebank (PKT-UD) to improve parsing model performance, specifically focusing on part-of-speech tags and dependency relations.
core_content.key_methodology: Manual revision of the entire PKT-UD corpus following UDv2 guidelines, including extensive changes to part-of-speech tags and dependency relations. The revised corpus was used to train a transformer-based dependency parsing model using biafﬁne attention.
outcomes: Significant improvement in labeled attachment score by 3.0% when using the revised PKT-UD v2020 corpus compared to the original PKT-UD v2018.
Robustness of parsing model improved due to more accurate and faithful annotations.
core_content.full_text_markdown: In this paper, we address important issues in the Penn Korean Universal Dependency Treebank (PKT-UD) by manually revising the entire corpus. The revisions follow UDv2 guidelines and include extensive changes to part-of-speech tags and dependency relations to reflect morphological features and flexible word-order aspects of Korean grammar. We experiment with a transformer-based parsing model using biafﬁne attention on both the original PKT-UD v2018 and the revised PKT-UD v2020 corpora. The results show a significant improvement in labeled attachment score by 3.0% when using the revised corpus, indicating that the revisions have improved the robustness of the parsing model.
